title: OptiMind Mechanism Record
description: Schema for a single mechanism record. Mechanism records live as anchor-ID'd subsections in comprehensive_memory.md (one per protocol-shaping causal claim). The mechanism is the *why* — universal-truth-scoped, changes when science changes, 1:m with protocols (one mechanism, many rules cite it via mechanism_ref — but each rule pins exactly one mechanism) and 1:m with sources (each mechanism nests its supporting citations in sources[]). This schema is the LOGICAL contract; in comprehensive_memory.md a record is RENDERED as markdown — `domain` is encoded in the anchor id segment and the enclosing §section, `claim` is the prose body, and `sources`/`last_reviewed`/`confidence` are the trailing `>` metadata line — so the literal JSON keys are not expected to appear verbatim. Introduced 2026-06-07 as part of the three-tier KB model (see CHANGELOG v4.1.0).

id.description: Stable identifier in the form mech.<domain>.<slug>. snake_case lowercase. The domain segment must match the `domain` field; the slug is the principle's short name (e.g. mech.sleep.thermal_onset, mech.nutrition.caffeine_pharmacology). Once published, this id is permanent — protocol rules' mechanism_ref pin to it.

domain.description: Top-level cognitive lens. Matches the four-domain framing in comprehensive_memory.md (§1 Neuro-Sleep / §2 Nutrition / §3 Psychology-Coach / §4 Strategy). Note: the ID uses the short form ('sleep'), not 'neuro_sleep'.

claim.description: One-paragraph causal mechanism prose. The why. Not the protocol (the what — that lives in the rule's `rule` field) and not the evidence (that lives in sources[] + journal). Should be readable in isolation — a future agent walking the connector should be able to verify the cited rule's why_brief against this claim without further context.

sources.description: Citations supporting the claim. Each entry is a free-text reference: published study (e.g. 'Haghayegh 2019 (Sleep Med Rev)'), journal entry pointer (e.g. 'journal:2026-06-06#11:18' — derivation log), or other addressable source. Mirror of the rule-level `source` field, hoisted to the mechanism (one mechanism, many protocols — sources live with the mechanism so they don't get duplicated across cite-sites).

last_reviewed.description: Bare YYYY-MM-DD (same convention as the rule-level last_reviewed). Per the re-validation invariant (CHANGELOG v4.1.0), mechanism records with last_reviewed > 6mo nominate for analyst re-validation — and since one mechanism may back many rules, a stale mechanism propagates the re-validation signal to all its referencing protocols.

confidence.description: Numeric only (matches the rule-level convention; no string enum form). Per the re-validation invariant (CHANGELOG v4.1.0), mechanism records with confidence < 0.95 nominate for re-validation.
